mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and real personal income growth indicate a synchronized collapse in residential and consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 45% over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of stable inflation reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%, or the mechanism yields negative alpha in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 5.0% combined with accelerating inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate demand and supports corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining housing starts signals a structural weakening in consumer and investment demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.5% and HOUST declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns, with 20-day forward returns remaining positive in > 55% of triggered cases during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable employment levels indicates efficient capacity expansion without labor market overheating, supporting positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns when UNRATE is below 4.5%, or the signal produces negative alpha in out-of-sample tests.
